name: Myanmar
headline: Myanmar FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — May 2026 pattern evidence sustained: junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Sagaing Region killed 22 students and 2 teachers (May 12); separate military jet bombings of two private boarding schools in Rakhine State's Kyauktaw township killed approximately 23 students. Amnesty International January 2026 documented 982 civilians killed in airstrikes in 2025 (53% YoY increase) including 287 children. HRW January 2026 documented 5-year-since-coup atrocities surge. Irrawaddy: 500 massacres in five years milestone. The May 12 Sagaing school strike pattern + May 17 confirmation of continuing airstrike pace = a defined educational-institution-targeting pattern (IHL-protected category). Already at floor (0.0). Floor sustained; documentation expansion via the school-airstrike-pattern category. The 'interconnected-floor-conduct-event' methodology category (May 16) — Rohingya stranded in Myanmar following India May 8 naval deportation — remains live as a carry-over.

AWR.rationale: Floor sustained. The school-strike pattern (Sagaing May 12; Kyauktaw separate incident) targets institutions with confirmed civilian-concentration profile. Awareness disregarded.

EMP.rationale: Floor sustained. 982 civilians killed in airstrikes in 2025 (Amnesty); 287 children among them; 1,728 civilians killed since late 2024 (HRW). May school strikes killed 45+ students across two incidents. EMP-dimension protective infrastructure absent.

ACT.rationale: Floor sustained. AC2 proportionality: airstrikes on schools have no military-objective justification under IHL — educational institutions are protected category.

EQU.rationale: Floor sustained. Differential harm to civilian populations including children. Rohingya stranded second-order impact compounds EQU floor.

BND.rationale: Floor sustained. IHL violations on civilian-shelter and educational-institution protection ongoing; armed drones, paramotors, gyrocopters in unlawful attacks per Amnesty.

ACC.rationale: Floor sustained. No accountability mechanism for junta airstrikes; HRW World Report and Amnesty external documentation, not actor self-accountability.

SYS.rationale: Floor sustained. Daily-airstrike pattern with educational-institution-targeting sub-pattern + Rohingya stranded second-order = systems-level conflict-driven civilian-harm pattern.

INT.rationale: Floor sustained. Junta governance posture incompatible with INT-dimension consistency framework.

evidence.summary: Junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Sagaing Region killed 22 students and 2 teachers (May 12). Separate incident: military jet bombings of two private boarding schools in Rakhine State's Kyauktaw township killed approximately 23 students.

Amnesty International January 2026: 982 civilians killed in airstrikes in 2025 (53% YoY increase) including 287 children. Junta increasingly using armed drones, paramotors, gyrocopters in unlawful attacks.
HRW January 2026: Myanmar junta atrocities surge 5 years since coup. 1,728 civilians killed since late 2024.
Irrawaddy: 500 massacres in five years — Myanmar junta hits gruesome milestone.
JURIST January 2026: rights groups censure escalating atrocities of Myanmar military junta.
Carry-over from May 16: Rohingya stranded in Myanmar after India May 8 naval deportation — 'interconnected-floor-conduct-event' methodology category remains live.

keyEvidence: May 12 Sagaing school airstrike: 22 students + 2 teachers killed; separate Kyauktaw school bombing ~23 students killed.
Amnesty January 2026: 982 civilians killed in airstrikes in 2025 (53% YoY increase) including 287 children.
HRW January 2026: 1,728 civilians killed since late 2024; 1,000+ civilian locations hit in 15 months.
Irrawaddy: 500 massacres in five years milestone.
Carry-over: Rohingya stranded second-order from India May 8 naval deportation.
Floor sustained at 0.0; school-airstrike-pattern (IHL-protected category) documented as recurring.
rationale: Floor confirmation at 0.0. The May 12 Sagaing school airstrike (22 students + 2 teachers) and the separate Kyauktaw school bombings (~23 students) establish a recurring educational-institution-targeting pattern within the broader civilian-targeting pattern. Educational institutions are IHL-protected; the systematic targeting of schools represents a distinct IHL-violation category. Already at floor — no downward movement available. Math: 8/8 = 1.0 → ((1.0-1)/4)*100 = 0.0.
watches: Continuation of daily-airstrike pattern into next reporting window
Educational-institution-targeting pattern: monitor for further school strikes
Rohingya stranded population: outcome and reception by junta-controlled areas
Civilian-targeting trajectory: 1,728 since late 2024 — pace acceleration risk
carryForward: Junta governance structural incompatibility with compassion infrastructure
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology observation: the educational-institution-targeting sub-pattern (Sagaing + Kyauktaw within May reporting cycle) is distinct from general civilian targeting because educational institutions are IHL-protected category. The methodology should formalize 'educational-institution-targeting' as a sub-anchor under BND-dimension IHL-violation scoring, weighted alongside hospital-targeting (Sudan) and IDP-camp-targeting (Israel). The Myanmar May 12 events are the canonical instance.
boundaryWatchResolution: Not a boundary case. Floor sustained at 0.0.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
